code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: ONLY a single number: 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5
    - Do NOT output any explanation, text, symbols, or punctuation
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
    - Explicit repurchasing/gifting behavior mentioned despite minor issues →5
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Multiple product failures within short timeframe
    - Dangerous defects or complete failure of core function
    - Explicit warnings against purchase + anger/returns
    - Updates negating initial satisfaction →1

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Significant degradation despite proper maintenance
    - Underperforms stated capabilities/competitors
    - Functional but requires excessive workarounds →2
    - Negative comparisons to superior alternatives →2

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with neutral/conflicted language
    - Temporary solutions with partial effectiveness →3

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Maintenance required after each use impacting convenience →4
    - Style/design praise with core functionality limitations
    - Explicit "good but not best" comparisons →4
    - Positive initial experience with unproven longevity →4

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Solves persistent problems others couldn't despite maintenance →5
    - Exceeds expectations despite initial skepticism →5
    - Maintenance presented as inherent tradeoff for core benefits →5
    - Long-term satisfaction mentioned despite minor quirks →5

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Worth the effort" acceptance →5 vs "Annoying but needed" →4
    - "Still works with quirks" updates →5 vs "Works but degraded" →4
    - Core problem-solving outweighs maintenance frequency →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "UPDATE: Replacement failed in 2 months. Never buying again!"
<Reasoning>Multiple failures with explicit rejection</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Underperforms Bissel - only surface cleans"
<Reasoning>Negative competitor comparison</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Works temporarily but needs replacement soon"
<Reasoning>Temporary adequacy without commitment</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Christmas napkins stay crisp but require ironing after each wash"
<Reasoning>High-frequency maintenance impacts convenience</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Between puppy messes and kids, this cleaner surprised me daily with effectiveness!"
<Reasoning>Exceeded skeptical expectations through core performance</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "These cloth napkins require ironing but their elegance justifies the effort"
<Reasoning>Maintenance accepted as trade-off for premium quality</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Effective scrubber but confusing controls. Deducted one star"
<Reasoning>Explicit star reduction for usability flaw</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Solved 5-year stain problem despite weekly deep cleans"
<Reasoning>Core benefit justifies regular maintenance</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Good value but less durable than premium brands"
<Reasoning>Direct comparison with material limitation</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "UPDATE: 1 year later, still perfect despite occasional jams"
<Reasoning>Long-term satisfaction with accepted minor flaw</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (36 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 14
  gold 1 -> 4: 1
  gold 3 -> 2: 1
  gold 2 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "If you're looking for a very budget priced bed skirt, this would be it. I have two twin beds in my guest room and after spending more than enough on all the other bedding and decorative pillows and such on them, I knew I needed bed skirts but hated p..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "There are two kinds of DII fabric storage bins - the ones that are reinforced and the ones that aren't. The ones that aren't tend to flop around and tip over. The ones that are reinforced are so much better. This one is the better style that actually..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "These mats are fine if you like to switch them out a lot. If you refer to have a very fresh and new bathmat for the bathroom when guests are staying for a visit, then these have a fun knobbly like texture to them and are cheap enough to pick up for s..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [3, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "Nice brush for cleaning the most difficult toilets. Mine are not that bad, but I wanted a scrubber that would last. I am sick of the rusting ones."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I can forgive the fact that there was blood on one of the math mats when I opened the package.  Things happen and I'm sorry that someone was injured.  What I can't get past is the fact that there are so many snags.  It's like these two mats were seco..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [2, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "Just got this in and so far, so good. It is lightweight and picks up the sand on my porcelain tile. Not many vacuums can get the sand from this tile and the grout and so far, this one has done the job.
UPDATE!!!! In April, the wheel came off and ther..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Returned. Only because I decided to go with tiles and not a rug"
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I've never tried an air purifier before, though I know people who have/had them. This one was not impressive. First, it has an odor. I don't know if it's because of impurities that emit a smell once trapped in the gadget and exposed to heat from the ..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 2, 1] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "If there's anything you ever thought you wanted or needed in a trash can, this one has it. First, it's a semi-round, so it seems to have a smaller footprint than traditional round cans. Second, it has a silent opening and soft closing, which is great..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 36 errors, 16 borderline out of 80 examples."}
